Rythmos® Door & Window Sensor

Technical Specification & Installation Reference · Rev 2026

Real-time monitoring
Propped alerts
Wander integration

Overview

Rythmos® Door & Window Sensor is a wireless magnetic contact sensor that monitors the open and closed status of doors, windows, and other access points, supporting wander management and facility security.

FUNCTIONS

Monitors real-time door/window status
Alerts on open or propped states
Supports wander management

Key Capabilities

Real-Time
Monitoring

Tracks
door and window state

Propped
Door Alerts

Detects
doors left open

Scheduled
Alerts

Alerts
only during set hours

Wander
Integration

Supports
elopement prevention

Wireless
Install

No
wiring required

Low
Maintenance

Long
battery life

Hardware Specifications

Dimensions: 51 × 24 × 15 mm
Magnet Dimensions: 30 × 10 × 9 mm
Power Source: 2 × AAA batteries
Battery Life: Up to 2–5 years (usage dependent)
Detection Type: Magnetic contact (Hall-effect)
Network: Rythmos® Mesh Network
Frequency band: 2.4 GHz
Radio sensitivity: −95 dBm
Typical node distance: Up to ~20 m between devices
Topology: Self-healing mesh (multi-hop communication)
Installation: Adhesive or screw mount
Firmware Updates: OTA (over-the-air)

System Topology — Door & Window Sensor Deployment

Install the sensor on all exit doors and other high-risk areas to ensure effective monitoring of movement and access. It can also be used for cabinets, restricted zones, and internal doors where additional control is required. Alert timing should be configured based on resident schedules to align with daily routines and reduce unnecessary notifications.

Installation Guidelines

Placement

- Install sensor on frame, magnet on moving surface
- Ensure proper alignment for accurate detection

Environment

- Indoor use recommended
- Avoid strong magnetic interference

Mounting

- Use adhesive for quick install
- Use screws for permanent mounting

Power

- 2 × AAA batteries
- Replaceable, no reconfiguration required

Alignment

- Maintain correct gap between sensor and magnet
- Ensure consistent positioning when closed

Conditions

- Op. temp: −20°C to +50°C
- Humidity: 0–100% RH (non-condensing)

Constraints

- Avoid proximity to RF sources
- Avoid direct sunlight and heating devices
